objective: Revise the article for tighter flow, less repetition, and better-supported implications while keeping the draft around 1,800 words.

assessment.summary: Draft has a strong thesis and stronger intro, but the middle repeated the browser-automation distinction, the copilots section was the most speculative, and several product / implementation claims needed narrower framing.

tasks: Narrow unsupported product claims instead of expanding sourced assertions in this pass
Keep implementation caveats concise and credible
Trim or caveat the strongest speculative claims

chunks.scope: Comparison + What WebMCP is actually for
Why WebMCP matters for AI browsers and assistants + copilots section
